issues.detail: The round-1 negative fixture covers a completed project but not a cancelled or formerly assigned/released project. An implementation filtering only `completed` can leak other non-current projects while satisfying the test, despite “current projects.”
issues.fix: Add cancelled and released/formerly-assigned decoys and assert that none appears in the current-projects section.

issues.detail: Only one current project is seeded, and the rewrite dropped the original “each matching its fixture” oracle. A dashboard returning only its first current project or displaying incorrect status, fuel, or Lovable-credit values can pass.
issues.fix: Seed at least two current projects and assert exact project-set equality plus each project’s exact fixture status, fuel balance, Lovable-credit status, in-progress-task set, and unresolved-blocker/clarification set.

issues.detail: The coverage map claims per-volunteer scoping, but AT-014.02 excludes only volunteer B’s projects. Because tasks and blockers/clarifications are separate sections, B’s task, blocker, clarification, or key-reveal control can leak while the test passes.
issues.fix: Seed sentinel tasks, blockers, clarifications, and key controls for both volunteers and assert that none of B’s data appears anywhere on A’s dashboard.

issues.detail: The [cx] phrase “the four STATED prohibitions only” omits the leading clause “completion-credit-only public reputation.” The test neither requires a public completion-credit representation nor excludes other public reputation primitives such as endorsements or rank labels.
issues.fix: Add a positive oracle for completion credit on the intended public reputation surface and assert that all public reputation fields/components are completion-credit-derived; retain the explicit profile, badge, star, and numerical-rating prohibitions.

issues.detail: “Survives byte-identical” exceeds “durable.” A compliant store may reserialize or migrate an event while preserving its identity and semantic fields. Also, “restart (reload fixture)” can merely reseed data and therefore does not necessarily prove persistence.
issues.fix: Restart the actual service/storage without reseeding, then assert the same event identity and exact semantic field values remain; do not require storage-byte equality.

issues.detail: The event-field oracle is incomplete: “expected first-tool eligibility” is self-referential, the completion timestamp is not compared with the controlled completion time, and the second appended event is not required to carry the correct second-project fields. Constant or malformed values can pass.
issues.fix: Pin both project IDs, the volunteer ID, controlled completion timestamps, and explicit eligible/ineligible outcomes, and assert all four fields on each event.

issues.detail: The out-of-band tamper probe modifies a stored event but does not exercise out-of-band deletion. A per-row checksum that detects edits but silently loses a deleted event would pass despite “non-deletable” and “tamper-evident.”
issues.fix: Add an out-of-band deletion probe and assert that integrity verification detects the missing event; keep the ordinary delete-rejection probe separately.

issues.detail: The test restricts the private “credit earned” confirmation to the volunteer’s first completion. The requirement attaches that confirmation to each per-project completion, so an implementation sending it only once can pass. “Records immediately” also lacks a defined observation boundary.
issues.fix: Separate the day-one first-completion case from the general case: assert that every completion has its event and private confirmation observable when the completion transition commits/returns, including a later completion.
